pattern_description: The simplest possible Chladni figure: two diagonal nodal lines forming a perfect X across the plate center. Sand accumulates along the two diagonals, leaving four triangular antinodal quadrants completely clear. The X arms run exactly corner-to-corner.
visual_profile: Diagonal X cross dividing the plate into four equal triangular zones
ai_art_prompt: Overhead view of a large square steel plate with golden sand forming a perfect X pattern, two diagonal lines meeting at center, four empty triangular zones, studio raking light from one side, macro detail of sand grain ridges, scientific photography style
cymatics_analog: Single concentric ring on circular plate at equivalent mode

pattern_description: A single horizontal nodal line divides the plate into two halves, with a partial diagonal producing an asymmetric T-like figure. Sand accumulates along one straight line parallel to a plate edge and a perpendicular partial line, creating two large antinodal regions and one smaller zone.
visual_profile: Asymmetric T-bar or L-bracket nodal pattern
ai_art_prompt: Square metal plate with sand forming an asymmetric T-shaped pattern, one strong horizontal line and a shorter perpendicular branch, unequal zones either side, raking side-light, documentary scientific photography
cymatics_analog: Diametral mode on circular plate with single diameter

pattern_description: Classic diagonal X pattern on a 30 cm aluminum plate. Two clean diagonal lines cross at the geometric center, dividing the plate into four roughly equal triangular antinodal regions. This is one of the most photographed Chladni figures due to its elegant simplicity.
visual_profile: Clean diagonal X, symmetric quadrants
ai_art_prompt: Polished aluminum plate covered in white salt crystals forming a perfect X, diagonal lines precisely bisecting corners, four equal triangular empty zones, overhead diffuse lighting, crisp scientific study

pattern_description: Four nodal lines — two parallel to each axis — forming a grid-like pattern with a central diamond or plus sign. Sand creates a square diamond rotated 45° in the center surrounded by smaller triangular antinodal regions in the corners and along the edges.
visual_profile: Central diamond grid with corner and edge antinodal lobes
ai_art_prompt: Large steel Chladni plate with sand forming a 3x3 grid pattern, central diamond rotated 45 degrees, nine antinodal regions showing as sand-free zones, side-lit to show depth of sand ridges, scientific macro photography
cymatics_analog: Double concentric ring on circular plate

pattern_description: A moderately complex pattern with five intersecting nodal lines creating an irregular geometric lattice. One diagonal and four lines parallel to the plate edges create asymmetric rectangular cells. Sand defines a beautiful irregular web with larger open regions near the plate center.
visual_profile: Irregular lattice with elongated rectangular antinodal cells
ai_art_prompt: 30cm steel plate with golden sand forming five crossing nodal lines, irregular web of rectangular cells, larger open zones in center, dramatic side lighting highlighting sand ridges, high-detail macro shot

pattern_description: On a small 20 cm steel plate the (1,1) mode produces the same X pattern but scaled to the smaller plate dimensions. The diagonal lines are well-defined and sharp, and the four antinodal quadrants are proportionally smaller. The tighter plate geometry produces crisper ridges.
visual_profile: Compact diagonal X on small plate
ai_art_prompt: Small 20cm square steel plate with pale yellow lycopodium powder tracing a perfect diagonal X, high-key lighting, minimalist composition, white background, scientific precision

pattern_description: The (2,2) mode on a 30 cm steel plate produces a central diamond shape with perpendicular nodal lines aligned to the plate axes. The pattern shows four lines in a hash (#) arrangement, with the center square containing the densest sand accumulation and four symmetrical corner lobes free of sand.
visual_profile: Hash-mark (#) pattern with central square and corner lobes
ai_art_prompt: 30cm steel Chladni plate showing a hash-mark symbol in golden sand, four straight perpendicular lines forming a central square, nine open antinodal regions, dramatic raking light, texture and depth visible
cymatics_analog: Square lattice on water Faraday surface at G3

pattern_description: Five nodal lines on a large plate create a visually impressive lattice with six elongated antinodal cells arranged in two rows of three. The pattern has a distinctly rectangular character and is one of the most commonly photographed of the mid-frequency Chladni figures due to its clarity and elegance.
visual_profile: Two rows of three rectangular antinodal lobes separated by a strong central dividing line
ai_art_prompt: Large 40cm steel plate with golden sand defining five nodal lines forming a 2x3 rectangular grid, six oval antinodal regions arranged symmetrically, warm side-light, grand scale scientific demonstration

pattern_description: A distinctive pattern with three parallel horizontal nodal lines creating a striped appearance. Sand accumulates in three parallel bands while the two intermediate strips remain clear. The outer edges of the plate also accumulate sand. Creates a visual parallel to staff lines on sheet music.
visual_profile: Three parallel horizontal bars of sand with two clear intermediate stripes
ai_art_prompt: Aluminum plate with three parallel white salt lines across its surface, two bright rectangular clear zones between, overhead soft box lighting, minimal scientific aesthetic, geometric purity

pattern_description: The middle-C resonance on a 20 cm steel plate. A straight horizontal nodal line bisects the plate, while a shorter perpendicular segment creates an asymmetric T. Two large oval antinodal regions on either side of the central divide are visible as clear zones, flanked by small triangular nodes in the corners.

pattern_description: Eight nodal lines — four in each direction — form a beautiful 4×4 lattice pattern with 16 antinodal cells. pattern_description: A 2×3 mode on a 30 cm plate producing six antinodal regions arranged in a 2-wide by 3-tall configuration. The five nodal lines are clearly defined, and the six lobes have a slightly oval quality with tapered ends near the edges. Classic music-box plate pattern.

pattern_description: Concert pitch A4 (440 Hz) on a 20 cm plate produces three parallel nodal lines along one axis, creating a four-stripe pattern. pattern_description: Eight nodal lines form a beautiful symmetric lattice on the aluminum plate. pattern_description: A glass plate at 880 Hz shows eight clearly defined antinodal cells in a rectangular 2×4 arrangement. The glass plate's higher Q-factor means patterns are exceptionally sharp and the nodal lines are narrow. The glass surface gives the pattern a crystalline, jewel-like quality when backlit.

pattern_description: A sophisticated pattern with ten nodal lines — three in one direction and four in the other — creating 12 distinct antinodal cells in a 3×4 matrix. pattern_description: The breathing mode of a circular plate: a single concentric nodal ring. Sand accumulates in one perfect circle roughly halfway between the plate center and edge. Inside the ring and outside it, the plate vibrates in opposite phase. The ring is uncommonly clean and symmetric.
visual_profile: Single perfect sand circle dividing plate into inner and outer zones
ai_art_prompt: 30cm circular aluminum plate with a single perfect ring of golden sand, plate center and outer rim both clear, overhead lighting emphasizing the ring geometry, meditative circular symmetry, acoustic physics

pattern_description: The simplest diametral mode: a single straight nodal line passes through the center of the circular plate. Sand forms a single diameter line from edge to edge, creating two semicircular antinodal regions. The line orientation depends on how the plate is excited — always a diameter but arbitrary angle.
visual_profile: Single diameter line bisecting the circular plate into two semicircles
ai_art_prompt: Circular 30cm steel plate with a single straight diameter line of golden sand, two clear semicircular zones either side, center-point support visible, low angle raking illumination, primal acoustic simplicity

pattern_description: Two diameter lines cross at the plate center, forming a symmetric cross or X on the circular plate. Sand accumulates in a plus-sign (+) arrangement, creating four equal pie-segment antinodal regions. This is one of the most visually satisfying circular plate modes.
visual_profile: Diameter cross (+) dividing circular plate into four pie segments

pattern_description: The second breathing mode: two concentric nodal rings nested inside each other. Sand forms two perfect circles at approximately 0.38R and 0.84R from the center (where R is the plate radius). Three zones — center, middle annulus, and outer annulus — vibrate with alternating phase.
visual_profile: Two nested concentric sand circles, three alternating radial zones
ai_art_prompt: Circular aluminum plate with two perfect concentric sand rings, three alternating clear and sand zones radiating outward from center, purple tinted diffuse light, target-like pattern, meditative geometry
cymatics_analog: Double-ring Faraday water pattern in circular bowl

pattern_description: The single diametral mode scaled to a smaller plate. The diameter line is sharp and perfectly centered. This mode on a smaller plate requires higher amplitude to observe, but the pattern is identical in form to the larger plate version — a fundamental property of scale invariance in plate modes.

ai_art_prompt: Small 20cm circular steel plate with a bold sand diameter line, intense red-orange side lighting casting long shadows from the sand ridge, dramatic scientific close-up

pattern_description: Two diameter lines at 90° on a 20 cm circular plate. The compact geometry makes the cross pattern exceptionally crisp. Four equal pie sectors are clear of sand. The plate edge accumulates additional sand around its perimeter. A deeply satisfying geometric figure.

pattern_description: Concert pitch A4 on circular plate! Three diameter lines at 60° create a six-petalled star or asterisk. Sand forms a six-armed star with equal 60° segments, creating six perfectly identical pie-shaped antinodal zones. One of the most beautiful circular plate modes.

pattern_description: Two concentric rings on the smaller plate are more tightly spaced than the large plate equivalent. The inner ring sits at approximately 7.6 cm from center, the outer at 17.0 cm. Three concentric annular zones are revealed — the innermost node is a small central disk, the middle an annular ring, and the outer a thin perimeter band.

pattern_description: Four nodal diameters at 45° intervals create an eight-armed star pattern. Sand forms an asterisk with eight equal arms, creating eight narrow triangular antinodal sectors. pattern_description: A mixed mode: two nodal diameters plus one nodal circle. Sand forms a cross pattern overlaid with a ring, creating eight distinct antinodal regions — four inner quadrants plus four outer arc segments. This mixed-mode pattern is among the more complex basic circular plate figures.

pattern_description: Five nodal diameters at 36° intervals create a ten-spoked wheel pattern. At this frequency only lycopodium is sensitive enough to reveal the pattern. The ten arms are delicate and narrow, and the ten antinodal sectors are small triangular wedges. Extreme geometric beauty.

pattern_description: The lowest mode of a hexagonal plate exhibits a single ring of six symmetric lobes — one along each edge. pattern_description: A distinctive snowflake-like pattern with C6v symmetry. Six pointed nodal arms radiate from the plate center toward the six vertices, and a secondary ring connects the midpoints of the six edges. This is one of the most visually stunning Chladni figures and is unique to hexagonal plates.

pattern_description: A broken-symmetry mode with C3v symmetry — only three-fold rather than the expected six-fold. Three strong radial arms alternate with three shorter ones, creating an asymmetric six-petalled flower. One of the most interesting hexagonal plate modes because it reveals the spontaneous breaking of the C6v symmetry.

pattern_description: A complex hexagonal mode showing an inner flower ring (six small inner lobes) surrounded by an outer ring of twelve lobes. pattern_description: High-frequency mode showing 48 micro-antinodal cells arranged in the characteristic hexagonal concentric ring geometry. pattern_description: A transitional broken-symmetry mode at C6 showing a striking pattern where three dominant hexagonal sectors each contain nested sub-patterns. pattern_description: The highest-frequency standard hexagonal mode in this catalog. On a 15 cm plate at C7, lycopodium reveals approximately 72 micro-cells arranged in the characteristic C6v hexagonal pattern. The cells are approximately 2 mm in diameter. The pattern is fragile — any air current destroys it instantly. A technical tour de force of acoustic visualization.
